mechanism_narrative: Rapid, large increases in the Federal Funds rate, implemented to combat high inflation, significantly tighten financial conditions and increase the discount rate on future corporate earnings, causing negative forward returns for the S&P 500.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.7 percentage points in a single month.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed hiked 75bps in June 2022, the largest since 1994.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts positive returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate, especially below 4%, indicates a late-cycle economy with maximum employment. This state fuels wage-push inflation, forcing monetary policy to become restrictive and thereby negatively impacting equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% is near multi-decade lows, indicating a very tight labor market.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the UNRATE level and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of slowing real demand growth and high, persistent inflation creates a stagflationary environment. This compresses corporate profit margins as input costs rise while consumer purchasing power erodes, leading to poor equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL growth > 5% and real year-over-year RSAFS growth < 2%
evidence_refs: fact:June 2022 CPI YoY was 9.1%, a 40-year high.
failure_condition: A strategy based on this interactive signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold S&P 500 strategy on a risk-adjusted basis over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and corporate activity. This signal of fundamental economic health drives positive S&P 500 returns, especially when the economy is in a recovery phase.
failure_condition: The signal's positive correlation with forward returns disappears or reverses (Rank IC < 0) during periods of aggressive monetary tightening (e.g., FEDFUNDS rising > 1.5% over 6 months).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, measured by the 3-month change in CPI, directly erodes the real value of future earnings and prompts expectations of further central bank hawkishness. This repricing of inflation risk leads to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Recent CPI prints in 2022 have consistently surprised to the upside.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in CPIAUCSL fails to show a statistically significant negative correlation (p > 0.10) with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts is a reliable leading indicator of a broad economic slowdown, as the housing sector is highly sensitive to interest rates. This signal of impending recession leads to negative equity market returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 15% from its 12-month high.
evidence_refs: fact:30-year mortgage rates have more than doubled from their 2021 lows.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts positive returns over the subsequent 60 trading days, indicating the signal is not reliable.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate from its cycle lows, occurring while the Federal Reserve is actively tightening policy, signals that restrictive monetary conditions are beginning to cause significant economic damage, presaging a recession and causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of UNRATE is > 0.3pp above its 12-month low AND FEDFUNDS > 1.0%
failure_condition: The S&P 500 does not experience a drawdown of at least 15% in the 12 months following a trigger of this condition in at least two-thirds of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A surprisingly strong nonfarm payrolls report indicates robust current demand and corporate hiring, temporarily boosting investor sentiment and leading to positive equity returns over a short-term horizon before longer-term inflation implications are priced in.
evidence_refs: fact:The June 2022 PAYEMS report showed a gain of 372,000 jobs, beating expectations.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following PAYEMS prints > 350k is not statistically significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
